title: elaeodendron novae-zelandiae
intro: Elaeodendron novae-zelandiae, also known as the New Zealand parasol tree or Parataniwha, is a small tree native to New Zealand with a broad, rounded crown and shiny green leaves. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and bark have been used for medicinal purposes, and it has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Elaeodendron novae-zelandiae are said to include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as improving cardiovascular health and immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
